content.full_text:
STATE OF CALIFORNIA                                    EDMUND G. BROWN JR., Governor


FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
1100 K Street
Sacramento 95811
(916) 322-5660


                                                    November 4, 1975


C. E. Jacobson, Executive Director
Traffic Managers Conference of California
5820 Wilshire Boulevard
Los Angeles, Ca. 90036

                                    Re:  Request for Opinion
                                         No. 75-189

Dear Mr. Jacobson:

     Thank you for your letter of October 15, 1975, request-
ing an opinion concerning appearances before the Public Util-
ities Commission. Since your letter raises no substantial quest-
ion involving an interpretation of the Act, no formal opinion
will be issued. However, I hope the following informal com-
ments will be helpful.

     The term "lobbyist" is defined in Government Code Sec-
tion 82039. The Commission has adopted regulations which
specifically address influencing or attempting to influence
administrative action in any public proceeding of a regulatory
or administrative agency, 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18239 (e).
A copy of that regulation is enclosed and your attention is
directed to subsections 18239 (e) (2) (C) and (D), and
18239 (e) (3).

     If you have any questions, please contact Nan Hambleton,
special compliance representative for the Commission.

                                        Sincerely,


                                        [signature]
                                        Daniel H. Lowenstein
                                        Chairman, for the Commission

DHL:pvp

TRAFFIC MANAGERS CONFERENCE OF CALIFORNIA

5820 WILSHIRE BOULEVARD       •       LOS ANGELES, CALIFORNIA 90036       •       TELEPHONE (213) 931-4073

Request No. ______75 159______

Date Received 10-[illegible]-75

Response Due [illegible] 11-3-75


October 15, 1975


Fair Political Practices Commission
1100 K Street
Sacramento, California 95814

Gentlemen:

Where a public hearing is held before the Public Utilities
Commission and a public transcript is recorded, is a representative in such a hearing, whether attorney or non-
attorney, required to account for time and expenses in
such a public hearing as a "lobbyist" under Fair Political
Practices Commission definition?

Very truly yours,

TRAFFIC MANAGERS CONFERENCE OF CALIFORNIA


[Signature]

C. E. JACOBSON, Executive Director


CEJ/djs
